top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer is anchored on the verse's chapter-closing function — eṣā as back-reference over 2.11 to 2.71 — preserved verbatim. The brāhmī sthiti and brahma-nirvāṇa technical terms are named verbatim with their metaphysical reach honestly tagged STRETCHED. The anta-kāle 'pi force ('even brief establishment carries through to the closing scenes of a career') is unique to 2.72. The 'twenty-five years in' chapter-closing scope is the engineering correlate of the verse's terminus-naming function. No other verse in the Gita combines the chapter-closing back-reference + brāhmī-sthiti + brahma-nirvāṇa + anta-kāle 'pi quartet.

BG 6.27 (praśānta-manasaṁ hy enaṁ yoginaṁ sukham uttamam / upaiti śānta-rajasaṁ brahma-bhūtam akalmaṣam) shares the brahma-bhūta theme and the highest-happiness state. But 6.27 is in the meditation chapter and lacks the chapter-closing back-reference function. Distant possibility.

BG 5.24 (yo'ntaḥ sukho'ntar-ārāmas tathāntar-jyotir eva yaḥ / sa yogī brahma-nirvāṇaṁ brahma-bhūto'dhigacchati) shares brahma-nirvāṇa terminologically. But 5.24 has no chapter-closing back-reference function and no brāhmī-sthiti. Distant possibility.
